title: Server/channel customization with role presets, richer profiles, username autocomplete, quick profile popovers, and fix avatar uploads

requirements.text: Add server and channel customization beyond basic create/edit/delete, including fields for server (name, description, icon, banner, settings as supported) and channel (name, topic/description, icon, settings as supported), with corresponding backend persistence and frontend forms to view and update these fields.

quotes: lets add the ability to configure, delete and customize servers and channels

acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user with appropriate permissions can open a server settings UI and update server fields; changes persist after refresh.
A signed-in user with appropriate permissions can open a channel settings UI and update channel fields; changes persist after refresh.
Server/channel list and relevant headers reflect updated names and images (icon/banner) when provided.

requirements.text: Implement a server role/rank permission system with preset roles (at minimum: Owner, Admin, Moderator, Member) and enforce permissions for server/channel actions (configure/edit/delete and any customization actions added). Include an initial path for server owners to customize role permissions after presets are created.

quotes: lets see with like a role and rank system if OK.
lets get presets, and after that server owners can customize further.

acceptanceCriteria: Each server has role assignments for members and at least the preset roles available.
Only authorized roles can edit/delete/customize servers and channels; unauthorized users see disabled controls or receive a clear error.
Server owners can modify the permission flags for preset roles (customization UI + backend persistence).

requirements.text: Expand user profiles to support additional fields spanning both a social style and gamer/tag style (e.g., bio, links, banner, status, badges, pronouns, theme color or similar), and update the Profile/Settings page UI to edit and save these fields.

quotes: maybe a little more to the profile page

acceptanceCriteria: Profile/Settings page includes editable controls for newly added profile fields and saves them successfully.
New profile fields render in at least one read-only display context (e.g., profile preview popover and/or a profile section on the settings page).
Validation errors (if any) are shown in English and prevent invalid saves.

requirements.text: Add a Profile/Settings toggle that controls visibility of the user’s ID (principal) to other users, and ensure this setting is respected anywhere the app displays user identity details (including the quick profile preview popover).

quotes: can you also add a toggle visibility of the users "id" in settings?

acceptanceCriteria: A signed-in user can toggle whether their principal/ID is visible to other users and save the setting.
When ID visibility is turned off, other users do not see the principal/ID in the UI (including in popovers); the owner can still see their own ID in settings.

requirements.text: Implement global username-based typing/autocomplete and resolution so users can quickly find/select other users by username/display name wherever user selection is relevant (at minimum: starting a DM, selecting recipients if applicable, and composing messages/posts for @-mention style selection if supported).

quotes: make sure that you can like type up usernames in places for easy access.

acceptanceCriteria: Typing a username/display name surfaces matching users in an autocomplete dropdown with stable selection.
Selecting an autocomplete result uses the correct underlying user identity (principal/userId) for actions like starting a DM.
Backend exposes a query suitable for user search that returns consistent results for partial matches.

requirements.text: Add a quick profile preview popover/modal that appears when clicking a user’s name or avatar in message contexts (channel messages and DMs at minimum), showing key profile info (avatar, display name, and newly added profile fields as appropriate) while respecting the user ID visibility setting.

quotes: click on the name/image of someone who sent a message and like a quick about of their profile pops up

acceptanceCriteria: Clicking an author avatar or display name in a message opens a profile preview UI.
The preview includes at least avatar + display name and a subset of expanded profile fields, and does not show the principal when hidden by that user.
The preview can be dismissed via click-away and/or an explicit close control.

requirements.text: Fix the profile avatar image upload flow so that uploading an image for a profile reliably works end-to-end (Profile Settings page and any initial profile setup modal), including proper validation, upload success feedback, persistence, and display via the existing avatar rendering logic.

quotes: i cant upload a image for my profile, it doesnt work. im not sure if the link image works (it might) but look into the upload image.

acceptanceCriteria: A user can upload an image file as their avatar, save, refresh, and still see the uploaded avatar.
Upload failures show a clear English error message and do not corrupt the existing avatar state.
Image URL-based avatars (if present) continue to work and do not break when upload is also available.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in backend/main.mo; only add backend/migration.mo changes if state migration is required.
Do not edit frontend files under the immutable paths listed in SYSTEM_CONTEXT (compose existing components/hooks instead).
Use English for any user-facing text.
nonGoals: Do not add third-party authentication providers (only Internet Identity).
Do not add real-time WebSocket chat; keep polling-based refresh.
Do not implement a fully custom role builder beyond editing permissions for the preset roles in this iteration.
